title: S1 Yearly Review + FY27 strategy planning
meeting_title: S1 Yearly Review + FY27 strategy planning

transcript.text: This meeting is being recorded.
How's it going?
Good.
How are you doing?
I'm good.
doing?
How you doing?
Sorry, this is so late for you all.
No worries.
Yeah.
I think it's the last month in the year.
I mean, not really last month, but the financial year.
So it's anyways quite a bit for us.
Yeah, I bet.
There's a lot going on.
All right.
Should I?
Here, I'll just jump in and share the agenda.
Yeah, Mahati is here as well.
Hey, Mahati.
Hello, everyone.

Okay.
Then I'll jump in.

Marketing priorities, any changes we should know about, you know, what measurements you, how your team sort of is measuring success and dial into all of that a little bit.
And then my hope is that we can kind of use both input from kind of our progress report, thoughts that you have on what's working and where you want to see improvement.
And then we can take all that away and come back to you, kind of review the strategy and then make any recommendations for areas that we might want to change or update the next time we get together.
So maybe what makes much sense, you can let me know, is for me to just jump in.
I did pull together sort of a high-level progress report.
So if you want, we could take a look at that, do a quick walkthrough, and then kind of move on to around more around kind of your upcoming year.
So I sort of put together a high-level overview, I think, with some things that we will dig into sort of in the second part of this meeting that we had talked about happening in early February.
So I think I'll save these sort of questions for kind of our alignment, but some of it's just a reminder.
of kind of the strategic goals from kickoff that's been six months now in terms of, you know, what metrics we're really focused on and kind of what success looked like.
So focusing on pipeline conversion, know, building LLM authority and also renewal capture.

And I recognize that we're still sort of in the early stages of our content program, but I do think that one place that for this coming year, we'll want to get more insights into kind of how your conversion pipeline works.

Kind of what data you're pulling from it, what events are important, and doing, I think, connecting our reporting and metrics more deeply to your conversion pipeline to understand how that's working, I think, would be a big priority on our end.
So then in terms of just the scope, obviously, we have these two lanes of content, the CVE database and then the editorial CS101 content.
So six months in, you know, we've published about 25 articles, and the CVE database is growing, and you're sort of holding the number one position, and LLM mentions that we're tracking via Scrunch.
We have that indexing issue, but once that's fixed, we should be close to 100% indexing.
So we'll keep a look on that weekly until we're kind of in a – we'll continue to monitor it weekly, but I'll let you know and report back sort of how it's looking.
So I
I would say things that need attention indexing, we talked about.
I would say the LLM competitive gap, not necessarily that it needs attention, but I think there's more opportunity to continue to grow there.

So I think that our strategy, we should look at from that in particular and see if there are kind of additional opportunities for content that might not be specifically SEO focused, but using kind of some of the learnings we're seeing across clients, you know, what's working in LLMs.

I think the other place that, you know, we could lean into more is that this sort of behavioral AA content, we haven't been doing nearly as many of these in this topic cluster, which, you know, contains a lot of the key differentiators, especially for kind of bottom of the funnel content and LLM, this could be kind of a powerful lever to test and see if we get traction there.
Or I'm saying.
I think we have a few articles in this topic cluster in the queue, so we could talk about prioritizing those.
We also, this could be a GA4 issue, but when I went in, for some reason, I couldn't access any engagement and conversion visibility before January.
So that was more of a question.
I wasn't sure if you had changed something in the GA4 setup or if it's just like a bug that I need to look into at our end.
But I will look into that and then I'll come back with more data on that and I'll take a look.
Yeah, what metric were you looking at for conversion visibility?
Let me see what our looker, what is it pulling from?
And then I can just share and show it to you.
So were you looking at direct dollar values or just a market of home fields?
Yeah, think we were looking at soft metrics.
I think often it's set up to track, it's not loading right now for some reason, events, like it's linked to the conversion events that you set up in GA4.
So the metric that we're seeing is kind of the events, so however you set them up, so, you know, first visits, engagements, session starts, those are the metrics that we are seeing.
Are you sharing your screen?
Oh, sorry, I am sharing the wrong tab.
So for some reason, we're not seeing, it's not pulling in any data before the current time period.

Event name.

Okay.

Okay.

here.

In terms of, you know, just site-wide performance, you know, traffic is stable with sort of, like, pockets of growth.

So I think keeping an eye on competitors, kind of narrowing the lead, but I think it's a couple points here and there.
So it's more just something to watch and think about what else we can do from a content strategy perspective to kind of help keep you in that position.
we can do So I

Maybe is that the great place so far?
What's working?

That's right.
So first of all, on the year in review, specific to CVE, I think it's more of the issue that we are dealing with today is more around a tech issue we'd probably solve.
I think one of the next best step from your team could be on Marcus where he could...

That the URLs with the trailing slash, that would in turn fix issues inside of sitemap, so that's a general site-wide change that the dev team did for good, but that kind of broke how vulnerability database was configured.
So I think a small fix from Marcus across the CVs should fix redirection issues, and then indexing should improve as well.
So he's working on that right now, so my hope is that after this meeting, I'll be able to just follow up with you on Slack and let you know when it's done.
All right, sounds good.
Yeah, I just wanted to make sure that there is clarity there with what's causing this and what's the fix here.
Yeah, I think we should be all good there.

Yeah, I think that fix should solve the issue and all the errors should get resolved inside of GSC as well.
Yeah, that sounds good.
And we'll continue to look and just make sure, but I'm glad it was a straightforward fix.
Yeah, and overall, for the engagement around vulnerability database, like we ideated, created it, launched it, and then we are inside of phase two at the moment.
I see a lot of newer CVEs published from your team that we are progressively publishing consecutively week on week to maintain both indexing and others.
But overall, I think it's been on the great side of what we have been doing with the vulnerability database as well on the content side.
Well, that's great to hear.
Yeah, we should be on track and updating it in the CVE release plan just to give you an overall sense of where we are.
So, you don't have have
Have to look at Airtable and see the thousands of entries.
But once we get this fixed, hopefully that should all be on track.

So, yeah, I guess the other questions around, you know, CS101, I know that at the beginning, at least when we started rolling out, it was a bit challenging to kind of find keywords, find our lane to operate in.

In general, you know, how is your team feeling about the topics that we're publishing, about, you know, how they relate to, you know, your team's strategy, kind of any changes you want to see going into 2026, or I guess your fiscal year, 2027?
Yeah, so strategically, I think direction is right.
We could probably do a session or more around the product line offerings that we have.
Probably we could bring someone from the product team, have you well, so that you, or the BMM team, so you know.
What exactly are the product lines and there's also a brand refresh ongoing that kind of clears what we are doing on the information architecture side of things.
So I think that clarity when brought to the growthx team specifically on the research side, that should refine.
And then I think more tailored content ideas could come specific to the product line we have at SentinelOne.
That makes sense.
Do you know when you plan, I guess, is the question around product and the brand refresh, are those connected?
Like, should we wait until that work is done to have this session or could we kind of jump dive into more of the product offering first?
We could jump onto the product offerings first, they're independent.
Okay, great.
And in terms of the, so I would love to, yeah, get a session on that on the calendar.
And then for the brand refresh.
Do you see that changing anything in terms of kind of our goals or messaging?
No, not really.
I think that's more on the information architecture side.
I guess Mahati can also comment.
Mahati, what do you think?
Does that really change what we focus on?
In terms of success criteria or business priorities?
I think either really is the question, since all both would inform the strategy we build.
Okay.
So I would say, you know, the success criteria is still the pipeline conversion and AI discoverability.
Those are the two main goals for this year.
And I'm focusing more on the engagement and conversion rate.
So conversion rate is going to be the metric that we will keep measuring for the website and all of the conversions coming through AI discoverability.
the the So,

But, you know, since these, the growthx and all the content lays over on top of the conversion, which is the first visit or the initial visit, I would say, yes, AI discoverability and LLM authority would be the next one.
So here's another question for you then.
know focusing on conversion, I feel like a lot of the content we have in producing sort of more top of the funnel, definitional content.

Or is that something that you feel like is owned by your other publications?
Because I know you also have your blog and a lot of other content you're producing.
Yeah, so we are expanding or redoing our entire products pages.
you.

Like, if it's coming purely from a CS101 page, how do we make sure that this is not somebody who has previously come to our page through organic and then later on converted through paid?
I think that's the attribution part is becoming a challenge.
Yeah, I'm sure, 100%.

Mostly I'm asking because, you know, we do have, you know, one of the groups that will sort of help shape this is, you know, technical SEO experts who have set this up and seen sort of for conversion tracking what's been working well across clients.
So just getting a sense of kind of what your baseline is, what you're tracking now, and what tools you're using and what data we might have access to can help us make further recommendations there.
Okay.
Yeah.
So right now we are tracking our average for FI 2026 was around close to 1.9 on a really good month.
And, but we started off less than a percent.
So we were, I thought, I think we were around like 0.9.
So we did, we did increase our conversion rate.
Our goal is to go above two and a half to hit three.
The industry standard is to go and a half.
For a technology SaaS company, but I want to go above two, two and a half percent.
Now, I'm also aware, again, the traffic coming into the site has drastically come down in the last one year, given the changes in the overall search engine and LLMs.
So if your team has actually solved how can solve the whole attribution part, if from your end, in terms of technical SEO team, I would love to get their help.
Like, I would love to know how they did it and what is the best way to make sure we have that tracking.
Because going forward, FGA is not able to track the full metrics, right?
So we want to understand how do we measure from the growth X side or how do we make sure that the attribution is coming through.
If it's if the.
The traffic coming into the website is coming from a different channel.
Yeah, no, I mean, I wouldn't say, I wouldn't want to claim to have solved anything as, you know, sticky as attribution.
But I think for sure, you know, the benefits we have is being able to see across accounts.
So I definitely have people who I can take this to and see if we can dig into it and help you get more clarity sort of into the metrics that matter to you.
So I will follow up with them as well as when we kind of talk about the strategy.

So I guess then one of the questions is, you know, in terms of LLM versus kind of more traditional SEO, when we think about the content we're producing, does one feel more important than the other to you right now?
Obviously, they go hand in hand, creating SEO content helps with LLM visibility, but sometimes there are, you know, opportunities to experiment, especially around.
I think very specific, like bottom of the funnel content that might surface higher intent traffic in LLM.

So just wondering kind of what your openness is to focus on kind of lower volume keywords that might be more useful for LLM or whether kind of our approach right now, which is kind of more grounded in traditional SEO, is the way that you want to proceed.
If there are ideas, definitely we would be open to it.
I'm not close to anything, actually.
Anything, if there's any other like radical ideas that could help us, you know, help us in any way, I would love to hear them and I would love to try them on.
Okay, great.
Then I will, we can definitely like think a little bit out of the box too.
I mean, right now the good news is you're owning the number one spot compared to, you know, your competitors, which is a good place to be in.
to
But, you know, there's always kind of think room to grow as that will, that traffic will get bigger and bigger and is more engaged.
So, let's see.
I think we sort of covered most of this.
I guess I wonder, you know, I guess, is, are there any changes into what you see as, you know, top marketing priorities or, you know, how your team is being measured?
Is there anything we should know?
Changes there over kind of how we're proceeding so far?
It sounds sort of like we're, that our top line metrics, those top goals are the same.
But if there's anything that's shifted, it'd be great to know.
So, last year, our main metric was organic traffic for the SEO team.
This year, it's, we're changing it to an overall complete, like that is web.
And you.
User testing and SEO across the board, it is one single goal conversion rate.
So depending on the amount of traffic that's coming to the site, we want to convert as soon as possible, quickly, in lesser steps.
How do we make it more efficient?
How can we convert the traffic that's coming from the CS101 and CVE?

Like explore areas on how we can have an ideal promos placed across CVE to convert them, even if it's like top of the funnel educational content or even free content, to somehow bring them from CVE into our site, into the high value pages of our site.
That's one of the main priorities.
The other is how do we stay ahead, right?
I know we are the one right now, but we are just producing content.
What else big can we go and do?
What are those ideas?
What is growthx?
Like, what can we do more with growthx is the question that I asked Ankit.
And I'm going to ask you the same.
What can we do different?
Like, what can we do other than just producing the basic, as you said, top of the funnel content?
What else can we explore and push ourselves to?
It would be the main goal for FY27.
Okay, that's great.
That is great feedback, I think, for us to think of, you know, right, making recommendations.
And also, I think, figuring out this push towards conversion and how we can do a better job, sort of showing how content connects to that as well, I think.
One really good, I would say, an interesting thing that happened yesterday, and I just wanted you to know, and I was going to let my executive boss know as well, is after the CVE database was...
Launched, looks like people are actually reading and valuing it.
On one of the vulnerabilities, there was a company that kind of, it was about that company.

In fact, they reached out to our community person on the community asking us to modify the CVE entry to say that they're actually seriously working on the fix and they will release the fix pretty soon.
So I thought that was like, oh, wow, this is good.
I mean, that company is actually reading what we posted and they're asking us to modify, like update our post.
So it makes them not like look good in the sense that they're also updating it.
So I felt good in terms of, okay, this is actually reaching not just the end users, but also the folks that have vulnerabilities against them.
So, no, I think that's a great, I mean, that, that feels like a great data.
And too, just to know that it's having like multiple layers of impact, know, that people are using it and getting it.
then people who own those care, care enough about the visibility to kind of reach out and ask you to change it.
So that's, I'm really glad you showed that.
That's awesome.
Yeah.
I'll share the email.
Ankit has the email.
Ankit, maybe if you want to share it, I think it'll be a nice success story or, or, or like an interesting story for you to, for you to tell.
Yeah.
Oh my God.
I love that.
I love that story.
And I think, you know, that's such a great example of, you know, programmatic content at a large scale can have a big impact.
So I think for us to investigate other recommendations we can make along those lines, I think would be great too.
It's like, I want to, you know, I want us to push ourselves to do something more radical and more out of the box instead of just generating plain SEO content.
Right.
I think that's where we could.
Have that extra leverage and stay ahead before somebody catches up to us.
Yeah, for sure.
Are there any kind of, I guess that question is such a, you know, a green field.
There's so much possibility.
Are there any, I guess, opportunities that you would want to flag for us as we go in and kind of think of other places we think we can get leverage against your goals?
Are there areas that you've thought of or have been curious that that would work?
Any starting points that you would, any places you'd want us to start?
Sure.
So, you know, more into my, my head is around like AI discoverability.
So it's not just the website.
How can we influence more outside?
I know there is, there is reviews, trust radius and all of that.
I have, I'm, I'm setting up connections on, on that end.
But, you know, places like Reddit, Quora, Discord, I'm, I'm seeing Discord a lot these days as well.
So places like these, how can we automate something or can we automate something to like, I found a Reddit monitor that will help me, you know, it's a tool somebody developed.
I found it on LinkedIn.
I spoke to the guy.
It's like a Reddit monitor that will take a look at every mention of SentinelOne and probably send me like an appropriate or places that we are mentioned.
And then I might have somebody resource go in and probably respond.
So something like that, like can we automate or AI, maybe semi-automate it in a way that could in turn help SentinelOne brand and discoverability.
I think that's one area which is outside the realm of the website that we could influence that will help the LLMs in AI.
Again, one point that I'm thinking.
So Reddit, Quora, Discord, are there other places?
I'm just learning more about LLMs and how they are getting the ranking and how their algorithms work and what would influence the results for us to be higher up in the citations.
Yeah.
No, that's all helpful.
And I think that kind of social listening across places like Reddit and Discord is at the top of everyone's mind a little bit right now.
And I know we have some people who have been digging into that.
So that'll be good to kind of for me to follow up and see kind of what we can do there.
I realize I'm unfortunately have another meeting right after this.
So my suggestion would be anything that you, is there anything you feel like we didn't cover for me to either follow up on async or kind of during our normal time?
Everything else I will bring back to our team and we'll kind of figure out the timeline to
I would say, like, review our work so far and then take a look at our strategy and recommendations based on kind of these updates from you.
Sure, sure.
Yeah.
Yep.
I would like to see some strategy for FI27 as we step into it.
February 1st is our first day of the new year, new fiscal year.
So something sooner would be great.
Okay, perfect.
I think I will let us and everyone know that that's kind of the timeline we're looking at, and I will follow up after this.
Okay, perfect.
Thank you so much.
you so much, everyone.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Review S1 performance and align on FY27 content strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/vFzRQoqZnYotnASj-_95TV4ddBrdJGPs?tab=summary&timestamp=110.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**FY27 Goal Shift:** The primary metric is now **Conversion Rate (CR)**, replacing organic traffic. The target is \>2.5% (vs. 1.9% FY26 avg).](https://fathom.video/share/vFzRQoqZnYotnASj-_95TV4ddBrdJGPs?tab=summary&timestamp=1484.0)
  - **New Strategic Direction:** The focus is on "radical," out-of-the-box content to defend the \#1 LLM position and explore off-site AI discoverability (e.g., Reddit, Discord).
  - [**CVE Database Success:** A tech company requested a CVE entry update, validating the database's real-world impact and authority.](https://fathom.video/share/vFzRQoqZnYotnASj-_95TV4ddBrdJGPs?tab=summary&timestamp=1607.0)
  - [**Immediate Fix:** A trailing-slash URL issue is breaking CVE indexing; a fix is in progress to resolve GSC errors.](https://fathom.video/share/vFzRQoqZnYotnASj-_95TV4ddBrdJGPs?tab=summary&timestamp=699.0)

## Topics

### FY27 Strategic Shift: Conversion Rate

  - [**New Primary Metric:** Conversion Rate (CR) is now the single goal for web, user testing, and SEO.](https://fathom.video/share/vFzRQoqZnYotnASj-_95TV4ddBrdJGPs?tab=summary&timestamp=1484.0)
  - [**Performance & Target:**](https://fathom.video/share/vFzRQoqZnYotnASj-_95TV4ddBrdJGPs?tab=summary&timestamp=1230.0)
      - [FY26 Avg. CR: \~1.9%](https://fathom.video/share/vFzRQoqZnYotnASj-_95TV4ddBrdJGPs?tab=summary&timestamp=1230.0)
      - [FY27 Target: \>2.5%, ideally 3% (vs. 2.5% industry standard).](https://fathom.video/share/vFzRQoqZnYotnASj-_95TV4ddBrdJGPs?tab=summary&timestamp=1230.0)
  - [**Attribution Challenge:** Tracking the true source of conversions is difficult, especially for users who visit via organic search and convert later via a paid channel.](https://fathom.video/share/vFzRQoqZnYotnASj-_95TV4ddBrdJGPs?tab=summary&timestamp=1173.0)
      - [**Action:** GrowthX's technical SEO team will investigate solutions.](https://fathom.video/share/vFzRQoqZnYotnASj-_95TV4ddBrdJGPs?tab=summary&timestamp=1283.0)

### S1 Performance Review

  - [**CVE Database:**](https://fathom.video/share/vFzRQoqZnYotnASj-_95TV4ddBrdJGPs?tab=summary&timestamp=699.0)
      - [**Issue:** A recent site-wide change to trailing-slash URLs broke CVE indexing, causing GSC errors.](https://fathom.video/share/vFzRQoqZnYotnASj-_95TV4ddBrdJGPs?tab=summary&timestamp=699.0)
      - [**Fix:** Marcus is implementing a fix today.](https://fathom.video/share/vFzRQoqZnYotnASj-_95TV4ddBrdJGPs?tab=summary&timestamp=711.0)
      - [**Success Metric:** A tech company requested a CVE entry update, validating the database's authority.](https://fathom.video/share/vFzRQoqZnYotnASj-_95TV4ddBrdJGPs?tab=summary&timestamp=1607.0)
  - [**CS101 Editorial Content:**](https://fathom.video/share/vFzRQoqZnYotnASj-_95TV4ddBrdJGPs?tab=summary&timestamp=590.0)
      - [**Performance:** 25 articles published; "AI threat landscape" is the top driver of traffic.](https://fathom.video/share/vFzRQoqZnYotnASj-_95TV4ddBrdJGPs?tab=summary&timestamp=590.0)
      - [**Opportunity:** Prioritize "behavioral AI" content to highlight key product differentiators and capture bottom-of-funnel traffic.](https://fathom.video/share/vFzRQoqZnYotnASj-_95TV4ddBrdJGPs?tab=summary&timestamp=333.0)
  - [**LLM Authority:**](https://fathom.video/share/vFzRQoqZnYotnASj-_95TV4ddBrdJGPs?tab=summary&timestamp=613.0)
      - **Status:** Holding the \#1 position for LLM mentions, with share growing from \~30% to 35%.
      - [**Risk:** Competitors are narrowing the gap, requiring a proactive strategy to maintain the lead.](https://fathom.video/share/vFzRQoqZnYotnASj-_95TV4ddBrdJGPs?tab=summary&timestamp=626.0)

### FY27 Strategic Direction

  - [**Product Alignment:** A session with the product team is needed to align content with product lines and the ongoing brand refresh (which is IA-focused and won't change goals).](https://fathom.video/share/vFzRQoqZnYotnASj-_95TV4ddBrdJGPs?tab=summary&timestamp=879.0)
  - [**Out-of-the-Box Content:** The team wants to move beyond standard SEO content to explore "radical" ideas.](https://fathom.video/share/vFzRQoqZnYotnASj-_95TV4ddBrdJGPs?tab=summary&timestamp=1726.0)
  - [**Off-Site AI Discoverability:** Investigate influencing LLMs on platforms like Reddit, Quora, and Discord.](https://fathom.video/share/vFzRQoqZnYotnASj-_95TV4ddBrdJGPs?tab=summary&timestamp=1775.0)
      - [**Example:** Automate monitoring and semi-automate responses to build brand presence.](https://fathom.video/share/vFzRQoqZnYotnASj-_95TV4ddBrdJGPs?tab=summary&timestamp=1792.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**GrowthX:**](https://fathom.video/share/vFzRQoqZnYotnASj-_95TV4ddBrdJGPs?tab=summary&timestamp=699.0)
      - [Fix the trailing-slash URL issue for CVE indexing (due Jan 21).](https://fathom.video/share/vFzRQoqZnYotnASj-_95TV4ddBrdJGPs?tab=summary&timestamp=699.0)
